expectations.text: Housing is identified as the largest expense category ($2,200)
expectations.evidence: Housing listed first at $2,200, noted as 'biggest by a wide margin'

expectations.text: Spending is broken down by account (Chase Checking vs Amex Gold)
expectations.evidence: Account Split section describes both accounts' usage patterns

expectations.text: At least one transaction is flagged as notable or unusual
expectations.evidence: 'The rent at $2,200 is far larger than any other expense - it alone is 66% of total spending'

expectations.text: Categories are ranked or sorted by total spend amount
Missing values or data quality issues are mentioned (even if none found)
expectations.evidence: No mention of data quality, missing values, or data completeness

eval_feedback.overall: Data quality assertion is a good discriminator between with_skill and without_skill.
